data.title: Chevron's win vs. Exxon lifts long-term growth prospects but cost time and billions of dollars
data.description:
Chevron's (CVX) arbitration victory over Exxon Mobil (XOM) and subsequent closing of its $53B acquisition of Hess (HES) helps solve one of Chevron's biggest problems - where its growth will come from beyond the next few years - yet the stock still ended Friday's trading -1%.

The completion means Chevron (CVX) now owns a 30% position in Guyana's prolific Stabroek block, which holds more than 11B boe of discovered recoverable resource; the stock had dropped 7.5% YTD on rising concerns over the company's financial and production growth prospects, with its reserves of oil and gas dropping to 9.8B boe at year-end 2024, the lowest in at least a decade.

Chevron's (CVX) organic reserve replacement ratio, which measures how much new oil and gas was added to reserves compared to the amount it produced, and which excludes acquisitions and sales, was just 45%.

Without Hess (HES), it was unclear how Chevron (CVX) could maintain free cash flow, American Century Investments portfolio manager David Byrns told Reuters, adding the acquisition will help Chevron sustain its dividend into the 2030s.

"Adding Guyana was critical for Chevron, because its go-forward portfolio was paltry outside of the Permian," Hedgeye Risk Management Managing Director Fernando Valle told Bloomberg.

Chevron's (CVX) production volumes after combining with Hess (HES) could top 4.3M boe/day in 2030, significantly higher than what the company would produce as a standalone company, Gerdes Energy Research's John Gerdes said; it produced 3.3M boe/day in 2024.

But Chevron (CVX) had originally targeted a mid-2024 close for the Hess (HES), so the delay was painful, sacrificing ~180K bbl/day of Hess oil, $6B-$7B in gross sales and $3B in profit from Guyana, "because those barrels kept flowing to Hess while the lawyers argued," Running Point Capital chief investment officer Michael Ashley Schulman said.

Meanwhile, Chevron's (CVX) much-needed win could prove "a blessing in disguise" for Exxon (XOM) and the government of Guyana, Welligence analyst Carlos Bellorin wrote, noting Exxon's dominant presence has been a political flashpoint for the opposition in Guyana, and Chevron's entry could dilute "the perception of a single foreign operator wielding outsized influence."

Chevron (CVX) will have to carefully navigate the situation with neighboring Venezuela, which has territorial claims on waters administered by Guyana, given Chevron's "significant footprint in Venezuela and its status as the country's most important commercial partner," Bellorin added.

Crude oil futures pulled back from early highs and settled lower Friday, giving up early gains from the European Union's announcement of tighter sanctions against Russia before sliding as traders weighed the difficulties of enforcement.

President Trump's threat earlier this week of secondary tariffs against countries trading with Russia unless a deal with Ukraine is reached in 50 days also had little impact, with gains limited by expectations of eventual oversupply as OPEC and non-OPEC countries raise output.

This week, front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for August delivery ended -1.6% to $67.34/bbl, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for September delivery settled -1.5% to $69.28/bbl; on Friday, both benchmarks fell 0.3%.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) closed higher and scored their first weekly increase in four weeks, with weather heating up and slowing the pace of inventory builds; the Nymex front-month August contract closed +7.6% for the week at $3.565/MMBtu, up 0.6% on Friday.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), -3.5% this week.

Top 20 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Critical Metals (CRML) +49.3%, American Battery Technology (ABAT) +42.8%, U.S. Antimony (UAMY) +42.3%, Energy Fuels (UUUU) +41.8%, Sable Offshore (SOC) +41.3%, MP Materials (MP) +40.1%, ASP Isotopes (ASPI) +39.5%, USA Rare Earth (USAR) +36.5%, Ramaco Resources (METC) +34.9%, Nuscale Power (SMR) +34.1%, VivoPower International (VVPR) +32.4%, Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) +26.8%, Lightbridge (LTBR) +25.9%, Idaho Strategic Resources (IDR) +25.2%, Oklo (OKLO) +23%, Uranium Energy (UEC) +21.8%, Centrus Energy (LEU) +20.8%, Denison Mines (DNN) +20.2%, Talen Energy (TLN) +19%, U.S. Goldmining (USGO) +18.7%.

Source: Barchart.com

data.title: Alcoa CEO says Canada growth projects on pause due to Trump's tariffs
data.description:
Alcoa (NYSE:AA) has paused work on all its growth projects in Canada due to President Trump's tariffs, and may need to turn to the Canadian government for help if the leveis remain in place, CEO Bill Oplinger told Bloomberg in an interview Friday.

Oplinger said he will wait until August 1 - the negotiation deadline for a deal between the U.S. and Canada - to decide whether Alcoa (NYSE:AA) will seek assistance from the Canadian government, financial or otherwise, to support the company's aluminum operations in Quebec.

"The longer this goes, the more damage it will do to the competitiveness of the Quebec assets," Oplinger told Bloomberg.

Alcoa (AA) is now "looking very hard at capital investments" in Quebec, Oplinger said. "The plans we had for growth projects in Quebec are on hold until we have some resolution on the tariffs."

While Alcoa (AA) has considerable production in the U.S., it also depends on operations in Canada to meet demand, owning three smelting and casting facilities in Quebec that largely feed U.S. customers, but now it is rerouting cargoes to other parts of the world due to the tariffs.

Nearly 40% of Alcoa's (AA) metal produced in Quebec can be diverted to non-U.S. customers, mostly in Europe and elsewhere in Canada, but weakened demand overseas has weighed on prospects for rerouting shipments entirely.

Oplinger’s comments follow Alcoa's (AA) earnings report this week that showed the company paid an additional $115M in tariff-related costs during Q2.

data.title: 3M reverses lower as adjusted organic sales growth seen as disappointing
data.description:
3M (NYSE:MMM) finished -3.6% on Friday, reversing lower after opening with a strong gain following a Q2 earnings topper and upwardly revised full-year profit guidance, as adjusted organic sales growth came in below expectations, according to Citi analysts.

Q2 adjusted organic sales growth of 1.5% was "modestly below our expectation and reflects still mixed demand trends across MMM's end markets (particularly consumer and autos and now electronics)," Citi analyst Andrew Kaplowitz wrote.

However, the analyst also said productivity actions and a focus on operational performance could drive profitability and let 3M (NYSE:MMM) hit its 2025 guidance.

Barclays analyst Julian Mitchell also noted that 3M's (NYSE:MMM) organic sales growth guidance has been cut to the low end of the previous range, which management had signaled.

Also, CEO Bill Brown provided a somewhat downbeat outlook on the economy and consumer sentiment during the earnings conference call, saying macroeconomic trends reflect "a global economy that remains sluggish and moving laterally, not materially improving or worsening," as reported by MarketWatch.

The CEO also said 3M's (MMM) consumer electronics business likely will soften in this year's H2 "due to slower demand for premium devices," the auto parts business will remain "challenged," and consumers likely will remain "subdued" through the rest of the year.

3M's (MMM) tariff burden lightened during Q2, with Brown saying the company now expects a gross tariff impact of $0.20/share for the full year, down from its prior estimate of $0.60/share, thanks in part to easing trade tensions between the U.S. and China.

"Things have stabilized at least a little bit," the CEO said on the call, adding that the company expects to offset half of the tariff's impact through cost cuts and price increases.

3M (MMM) faces significant potential legal liabilities, and is generating less cash, both of which increase its risk, said Edward Jones analyst Faisal Hersi, who has a Sell rating on the stock.

Litigation alleging groundwater pollution could take several years to resolve and potentially result in billions of dollars in liabilities, Hersi says, believing the stock will trade on litigation-related news, rather than company fundamentals: "If 3M receives a negative ruling from its ongoing litigation, there could be significant downside for the stock."

data.title: Gold futures finish flat week as rally loses steam
data.description:
Gold futures edged higher Friday thanks to a weaker U.S. dollar and ongoing economic uncertainty to close out a flat week, with the metal remaining stuck in a narrow range as the market waits for clarity around U.S. trade policy, tariffs and the outlook for rate cuts.

Gold’s impressive YTD rally seems to be running out of steam, as the metal has traded broadly sideways even though the U.S. dollar weakened to its lowest level against the euro in four years at the beginning of July, Commerzbank analysts wrote.

Sharp increases in the prices of silver, platinum and other precious metals over the same time period indicate traders see little further upside in gold and are looking for alternatives - hardly surprising after gold gained nearly 27% YTD, mostly in the first few months of the year, the bank said.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for July delivery finished -0.1% to $3,353.00/oz this week, and front-month Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) for July delivery ended -1.2% on the week to $38.22/bbl; on Friday, both metals gained 0.4%.

Palladium (XPDUSD:CUR) closed down 1.6% on Friday to $1,259.09 after reaching its highest level since 2014 in the previous session.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: BuildDirect announces private placement of up to C$7 million
data.description:
• BuildDirect.com Technologies (TSXV:BILD:CA) announced on Friday a non-brokered private placement of up to 6,100,000 common shares at C$1.15 per common share for total gross proceeds of up to C$7 million.
  • The company will use the net proceeds from the offering to continue to advance BuildDirect's growth strategy and for general working capital purposes.
  • The company said its three largest shareholders - Pelecanus Investments, Lyra Growth Partners and Beedie Investments - will participate in the offering. 
  • BILD:CA closed +1.93% at $1.58.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Block surges nearly 10% on S&P 500 inclusion
data.description:
Shares of Block (NYSE:XYZ) jumped nearly 10% in extended trading on Friday on the announcement that the fintech firm will be added to the constituents of the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500).

According to S&P Dow Jones Indices, Block will replace Hess (NYSE:HES) in the S&P 500, effective before the opening of trading on Wednesday, July 23. Hess (NYSE:HES) will be acquired by Chevron (CVX) in a deal that closed on July 18.

Block lost nearly 15% so far this year, compared to the 7% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Alphabet earnings, McGraw Hill IPO, Trump talks AI, and more robotaxi buzz
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - July 21

  • Volatility watch - Northern Dynasty Minerals (NAK) and Asset Entities (ASST) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Monogram Technologies (MGRM), Cogent Biosciences Inc (COGT), and Vigil Neuroscience (VIGL). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Intapp (INTA) and NextNRG (NXXT).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Verizon (VZ), NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), and Domino's Pizza (DPZ).
  • IPO watch - The IPO calendar heats up with global education company McGraw Hill (MH), global consumer intelligence company NIQ Global Intelligence (NIQ), and insurance player Accelerant (ARX) all expected to start trading.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Caterpillar (CAT), Dell Technologies (DELL), Lowe's Companies (LOW), and Pfizer (PFE). 
  • All day - Axsome Therapeutics (AXSM) will host its Frontiers in Brain Health R&D Day. The event will highlight Axsome’s singular, innovative late-stage CNS pipeline.

 

Tuesday - July 22

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Coca-Cola (KO), Philip Morris International (PM), Texas Instruments (TXN), General Motors (GM), and Lockheed Martin (LMT).
  • 8:30 a.m. WTI crude July futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • 8:30 a.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell gives welcome remarks before hybrid "Integrated Review of the Capital Framework for Large Banks Conference" hosted by the Federal Reserve
  • 1:00 p.m. Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman will host a fireside chat to discuss effects of artificial intelligence on banks, businesses, and consumers

 

Wednesday - July 23

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Alphabet (GOOG), Tesla (TSLA), IBM (IBM), T-Mobile US (TMUS), AT&T (T), Chipotle (CMG), Hilton Worldwide (HLT), and General Dynamics (GD). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Alaska Air (ALK) and Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) after their earnings are released.
  • All day - President Trump is scheduled to give a speech on AI. Traders expect extra volatility for Bitcoin (BTC-USD), Ethereum (ETH-USD), Solana (SOL-USD), and XRP (XRP-USD) into the speech.
  • All day - Circana will release its June report on video game hardware and software sales. Sony's (SONY) PlayStation 5 was the best-selling hardware platform in May for both units and dollars.
  • All day - Faraday Future Intelligent (FFAI) will host a private reception at the Capitol Hill Club in Washington, D.C., to highlight U.S. innovation and alignment with current trade policy goals.
  • 10:00 a.m. The Atlanta Fed Business Inflation Expectations update will be released. The report is a monthly survey conducted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta to assess firms' expectations of future inflation and their current business conditions.
  • 1:00 p.m. The National Retail Federation will hold a back-to-school webinar featuring insights on what to expect this shopping season.
  • 5:30 p.m. Tesla (TSLA) will hold its earnings conference call, during which Grok is expected to make an appearance. Elon Musk has said Tesla vehicles and the Optimus V3 humanoid robot are now equipped with the generative AI chatbot. Robotaxi news from Tesla (TSLA) could be relevant to companies such as Uber (UBER), Waymo (GOOG), WeRide (WRD), Pony AI (PONY), Zoox (AMZN), and Lucid Group (LCID).

 

Thursday - July 24

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Honeywell (HON), Union Pacific (UNP), Blackstone (BX), Intel (INTC), Deutsche Bank (DB), and Newmont (NEM). Options trading implies a double-digit move for IMAX (IMAX) and Boston Beer (SAM) after their earnings are released.
  • All day - Global flash PMI will be the focus of economists in an early gauge of the impact of the tariffs.
  • 7:45 a.m. The European Central Bank will release its monetary policy statement. ECB officials will also hold a press conference
  • 10:00 a.m. The New Home Sales report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
  • 4:30 p.m. The Nasdaq short interest report will be released.

 

Friday - July 25

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include HCA Healthcare (HCA), Charter (CHTR), Phillips 66 (PSX), and Centene (CNC).
  • All day - The Alzheimer's Association International Conference will begin over the weekend in Toronto, Canada. It is the largest international conference dedicated to advancing dementia science. Some of the companies that will present include Acumen Pharmaceuticals (ABOS), Cerevance (CRVN), Marvel Biosciences (MRVL:CA), and Quanterix (QTRX).
  • 8:30 a.m. The June Durable goods order report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau. Economists forecast a durable goods order decline of 10.0% for the month.

data.title: Linkage Global announces $3.5M convertible note financing
data.description:
• Linkage Global (NASDAQ:LGCB) announced on Friday that it has entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement for the issuance and sale of senior unsecured convertible notes in an initial principal amount of $3.5 million in a private placement.
  • The company expects to receive gross proceeds of $2.7 million at the initial closing. An additional $450,000 is expected to be funded within one business day following the effectiveness of a registration statement registering the resale of the underlying conversion shares.
  • The convertible notes are senior unsecured obligations of the company and were sold at 90% of their principal amount. They are convertible into the company’s Class A ordinary shares, par value $0.0025 per share.
  • The financing establishes a capital framework for additional issuances of up to $26.5 million in principal amount of similar convertible notes in future tranches. 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including expansion of its cross-border sales operations and integrated e-commerce service lines.
  • LGCB +0.51% after hours to $2.42.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Beneficient receives Nasdaq listing determination notice
data.description:
• Beneficient (NASDAQ:BENF) on Friday said it was notified by Nasdaq that, due to its continued non-compliance with the minimum $1.00 bid price requirement set forth in listing rules and the delay in the filing of Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025 with the SEC, the company’s securities were subject to delisting unless the company timely requests a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel.
  • The company plans to timely request a hearing and a stay of any suspension action by Nasdaq, it said.
  • BENF -4.95% after hours to $0.3301.
  • Source: Press Release
